raw_consumer_statement:
A purchase was made via XXXX and the credit card associated with my account using Barclays XXXX XXXX  on Sunday morning XX/XX/2025. 
I contacted XXXX, as well as Barclays, as I did not recognize the amount of {$3500.00} being charged to my credit card. I originally thought it was a scam until the charge was shown on my credit card. 

The item which was purchased from XXXX, was a 'domain ' name ( XXXX ). After considerable time with XXXX on the phone within days, I found that the purchase was made by my XXXX XXXX XXXX grandson, using my XXXX with my saved login and payment credentials. 

I have contacted XXXX, Barclays as well as XXXX. They will not reverse the charge of {$3500.00} as they said it was a 'domain ' name which was being sold by XXXX and that XXXX was the 'middle man ' selling the domain for someone else. Ironically, I received an email several days later which XXXX offered to buy the domain for {$1600.00} I have filed complaints with XXXX, XXXX as well as Barclays. They all have denied my request to reverse the charges even though it was an unauthorized purchase made by a XXXX XXXX XXXX, who can't read and has no concept of money. 

HELP!
